heroHeadline: Day 32: 'El 03' Consolidates CJNG Leadership; Rival Cartels Press Michoacán and Guanajuato
heroSubtitle: Day 32 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa. U.S.-born Juan Carlos Valencia González ('El 03'), stepson of the late Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, has been confirmed by the U.S. National Counterterrorism Center and State Department as the de facto leader of CJNG, with a $5 million U.S. reward for his capture. The cartel is operating under a collective council structure as 'El 03' consolidates power — but rival organizations Carteles Unidos, Santa Rosa de Lima, and Sinaloa factions are intensifying pressure on CJNG territory in Michoacán, Guanajuato, and along the Pacific coast. Drug trafficking operations at the ports of Manzanillo and Lázaro Cárdenas are assessed to be continuing under existing operational cadres despite the succession vacuum.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 26, 2026.
